1989_10_22-India-Bombay-Meditation_on_the_month_of_Damodar-st2-vr9

Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

MetaData v8

Event Name/Title

Meditation on the month of Damodar

Event Date

22 Oct 1989

Event Location

India, Bombay,

Event Type

Temple - BG class

Transcriber

Sri Varshabhanavi devi dasi

Completed

24 May 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-076-Meditation%20On%20The%20Month%20Of%20Damodar%20(Mumbai)-BG_4_9-dt_89_10_22.mp3

Source Video

Verse

BG 4.9

Tags – Places

Vrindavan, Mumbai.

Tags – Pastimes

Damodar Lila

Tags – Persons

Krishna, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Mother Yashoda, Maharaj Nanda, Shrila Prabhupada

Tags – Themes

Becoming dear to Krishna, The Peace formula, The nature of a devotee, Relationship of Lord with living entities.

janma karma ca me divyam

evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ

tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma

naiti mām eti so ’rjuna

[Bg. 4.9]

Translation

One who knows the transcendental nature of My appearance and activities does not, upon leaving the body, take his birth again in this material world, but attains My eternal abode, O Arjuna.

Purport

The Lord’s descent from His transcendental abode is already explained in the sixth verse. One who can understand the truth of the appearance of the Personality of Godhead is already liberated from material bondage, and therefore he returns to the kingdom of God immediately after quitting this present material body. Such liberation of the living entity from material bondage is not at all easy. The impersonalists and the yogīs attain liberation only after much trouble and many, many births. Even then, the liberation they achieve – merging into the impersonal brahma-jyotir of the Lord – is only partial, and there is the risk of returning to this material world. But the devotee, simply by understanding the transcendental nature of the body and activities of the Lord, attains the abode of the Lord after ending this body and does not run the risk of returning to this material world. In the Brahma-saṁhitā (5.33) it is stated that the Lord has many, many forms and incarnations: advaitam acyutam anādim ananta-rūpam. Although there are many transcendental forms of the Lord, they are still one and the same Supreme Personality of Godhead. One has to understand this fact with conviction, although it is incomprehensible to mundane scholars and empiric philosophers. As stated in the Vedas (Puruṣa-bodhinī Upaniṣad):eko devo nitya-līlānurakto,bhakta-vyāpī hṛdy antar-ātmā

“The one Supreme Personality of Godhead is eternally engaged in many, many transcendental forms in relationships with His unalloyed devotees.” This Vedic version is confirmed in this verse of the Gītā personally by the Lord. He who accepts this truth on the strength of the authority of the Vedas and of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and who does not waste time in philosophical speculations attains the highest perfectional stage of liberation. Simply by accepting this truth on faith, one can, without a doubt, attain liberation. The Vedic version tat tvam asi is actually applied in this case. Anyone who understands Lord Kṛṣṇa to be the Supreme, or who says unto the Lord, “You are the same Supreme Brahman, the Personality of Godhead,” is certainly liberated instantly, and consequently his entrance into the transcendental association of the Lord is guaranteed. In other words, such a faithful devotee of the Lord attains perfection, and this is confirmed by the following Vedic assertion:tam eva viditvāti mṛtyum eti,nānyaḥ panthā vidyate ’yanāya

“One can attain the perfect stage of liberation from birth and death simply by knowing the Lord, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and there is no other way to achieve this perfection.” (Śvetāśvatara Upaniṣad 3.8) That there is no alternative means that anyone who does not understand Lord Kṛṣṇa as the Supreme Personality of Godhead is surely in the mode of ignorance and consequently, he will not attain salvation simply, so to speak, by licking the outer surface of the bottle of honey, or by interpreting the Bhagavad-gītā according to mundane scholarship. Such empiric philosophers may assume very important roles in the material world, but they are not necessarily eligible for liberation. Such puffed-up mundane scholars have to wait for the causeless mercy of the devotee of the Lord. One should therefore cultivate Kṛṣṇa consciousness with faith and knowledge, and in this way attain perfection.

Lecture Transcription

Om namho Bhagavate Vasudevaya [Devotees:] Om namho Bhagavate Vasudevaya

Perfection of Happiness

[Maharaj:] In the fourth chapter of Bhagavad Gita, Lord Sri Krishna narrates one of the most important of all verses in all the Vedic literatures. In this particular verse, the secret of how to attain the perfection of happiness is revealed. [Maharaj reads the verse] Krishna says that, “One who understands the transcendental nature of my appearance and activities, never has to take birth again in this material world, but attains to the eternal abode, the supreme abode of the Lord.”

This particular month, the month of Karthik, is considered to be the most auspicious month of the year by many great saints and sages. Sometimes, this is also called the month of Damodar. Damodar is that form, the aspect of Lord Sri Krishna ‘s unlimited personality wherein he appears in the form of a beautiful baby Yashoda Nandana, Nanda Suta, the beautiful darling child of Gokul. So, one may ask how is it that you’re worshipping God, who is the Supreme Father of all living beings, as a baby? Ha!

Conception about God

In the Western religions, the Islam region, the Christian religion, the Jewish religion, they have a very, very grave and wrathful concept of God. In the sixteenth chapel, in Vatican square, in Rome, we have been there and many of you, art lovers have certainly seen pictures, and the ceiling of this sixteenth chapel, the greatest of all western artist Michael Angelo. He painted a beautiful depiction of the heavens that hails in the earth and in the middle, there is a picture of God. God is reaching his hand out to man and their fingers are just touching and, in this way, God is investing life through his mercy into man. He looks like the grandfather you always wanted, if you are a good grandson. But if you are naughty grandson, he looks like the grandfather you never you… you never want. He is very serious and you do not want to disobey this grandfather just by looking at him, you never want to disobey, because we read in the Torah, The Old Testament, the New Testament, the Quran, we read how if you disobey this very great, great, great grandfather of all living beings, Ha! he becomes very angry.

In the Old Testament, one of the most famous stories is a story of two cities, the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. Do you all know that story? These two cities were actually, originally very holy places, ah? but in course of time, like in most places in this world, people became very, very degraded. They became absolutely committed to all sinful activities and habituated. And the nature of sinful activities is when you commit them, you become more and more under the influence of Lust and Envy and Anger and Greed and Pride and it gets worse and worse and worse. Therefore, these people became so absolutely degraded that the only standard of pleasure was the most horrible obscene sex life with no consideration of what the opposite sex is, without consideration of marriage, anything goes. To the degree the immorality of sex exchange becomes degraded, without discrimination, to that degree we can understand how religious or irreligious is the society. So, God spoke to one of his devotees of the name Laach, he told him, “Get out of these two cities because I am about to teach them a lesson.” So, they left and then the Wrathful God he... he hurled down fire in Brimstone and he just completely demolished the city, burnt it to ashes and killed everybody. He was very angry. So, this is the fact. The conception of Almighty God in most of the religions of the world. And therefore, people are taught to fear God., “If you do not obey the law of God, then his wrath will be upon you.” Ha! In most of the Western religions, young children are taught to fear God that, “You are a sinner. You have sinned. From your birth you have sinned. From your conception you have sinned. Therefore, repent or burn in hell forever. God is a wrathful God. He is an en… he is a jealous God. Amen.” Ha! People are like this. And we read in the Shrimad Bhagavatam about that same Supreme God and he is in the beautiful form of a little blue baby Haa? with lotus eyes, very playful, likes to dance Haa? The Gopis, they used to take this little baby Gopal and they would say, “Gopal dance.” And he was just hardly walking, and then they would all clap their hands like this [Maharaj claps] and they would all chant beautiful songs in his praise Hare Krishna Hare Krishna. And he would start dancing and everyone also would laugh, like a puppet in the hands of these young girls, he was just dancing, just to please them, just to make them happy. So, one may think ‘What is this? Two completely opposite conceptions of God. Does this mean that one religion is correct and one religion is incorrect?’ Ah! That is what most religious people think today that ‘I have a certain conception of God and I call him by certain names, and if you have any other name or any other conception then you are going to hell. Ahha ! and I am not.’

One time I was speaking on an American university campus and one person came to me, he said, “What is God’s name?” I said, “God has many names.” He said, “What do you call God?” I said, “I call God many names.” He said, “What are you chanting?” I said, “I am chanting Hare Krishna.” “What is this Hare Krishna?” “It is the name of God.” “Ha! You are going to hell.” I said “Acha! I am going to hell. Why am I going to hell?” “Because you call God, I am not even going to say that Name, but you said it, that’s not his name.” So, I said, “What is his name?” Ahan! He said, “We have one savior, and if you don’t accept that savior and you don’t accept his name then, you must go to hell forever.” Hm! So, I sat there and he said, “So, are you going to convert to my religion or you are going to go to hell?” [Devotees laugh] I asked him, “Before I make a decision, because you know this is a very serious decision. You are asking me to make on the spot. [devotees laugh], I have to know one thing, my dear sir, where are you going?” He said, “I am going to heaven.” “Han! That’s all I wanted to know. Then, I would rather go to hell.” [devotees laugh]. He said, “What? You want to go to hell?” I said, “No offense, but if heaven is full of people like you with such closed minds, such hateful, wrathful people then I think I will probably be much happier in hell, there is probably much more open minded, religious people there.”

General Idea about God

So, you see, sometimes there is this confusion Ah? My God is there ever this confusion. If you read the newspapers, the Sikhs and the Hindus are killing each other, the Buddhists and the Hindus are killing each other, the Muslims and Hindus are killing each other, but don’t feel so bad because the Muslims and Muslims are killing each other and the Muslims and Christians in Beirut, they are killing each other and the Christians and Jews are killing each other and in Israels, the Muslims and the Jews are killing each other. And in Irelands, the Catholic and Protestant are killing each other and in my proud country America, the country, the home of the free and the land of the brave, America everyone is killing each other [devotees laugh] Han! It’s about one thousand denominations of Christians, everyone considers that we are the only way and every other person is going to hell. This is the general idea. So how do we understand that in one scripture God is very wrathful, throwing fire and Brimstones and thunderbolts and in another, he is stealing butter, Makhanchor Han? [Maharaj laughter] and when he sees his mother coming, he becomes very afraid, he runs, runs away afraid that she will get ang… she might punish him Ah? You see, Krishna says in Gita

ye yathā māṁ prapadyante

tāṁs tathaiva bhajāmy aham

mama vartmānuvartante

manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ

[Bg. 4.11]

[As all surrender unto Me, I reward them accordingly. Everyone follows My path in all respects, O son of Pṛthā.]

‘As you approach me, I reward you accordingly.’ Ah? Just like a father, Krishna says in Gita ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā [Bg. 14.4] I am the seed giving father of all living beings. Now if a father has several sons and one of the sons is very, very disobedient, inconsiderate and just outright naughty. He is basically a grown son, he knows what's right and wrong, but he is constantly engaging in activities which the father knows, if he continues will completely destroy his life. Now how does the father deal with such a son? First, he encourages, “Please my boy, do not behave like this, and it is not the right way, do as I am doing.” But the son goes out, he takes, steals father’s money, he buys drugs, he engages in all illicit activities. Ultimately the father has only one way to deal with his son, by punishing him. “If you do this, I will… I will beat you up very bad. I will not give you any facilities, I will not allow you to use the house, I will not give you any money. I will never disown you, but I will punish you very bad.” Now why is he taking such a position? Out of love, because he knows that if the child continues like that, the child will ruin his own life, so out of love, he is punishing, he is threatening. So, for disobedient people, God appears like that. But for the very obedient son, whose only desire is to please the father. Does the father threaten him? Does the father punish him? No. The father, the most intimate desires and activities in the father’s life, he shares with his beloved son. Now this father is equal to both sons, he is willing to shares his life with both. But, one son is willing to share with father’s love and the one son is simply as we say a ‘rascal’. So, therefore the love of the father appears in two different ways. God is infinite, God is unlimited, God can appear in multifarious ways and in each and every particular way he is showing his particular type of love which will both... best benefit his children. So, to those pure devotees, who have completely surrendered their life to the Lord, the lord reciprocates by dealing with them in the most intimate and pleasing way.

Significance of Damodar month

So, in this particular month of Damodar, it is suggested by the great sages that those devotees of Lord Sri Krishna remember his beautiful pastimes as a baby every day, particularly that pastime, by which he gains the name Damodar. So, for your purification, on this auspicious night, in this auspicious month, I would like to narrate you this beautiful story found in the Shrimad Bhagavatam and also try to give you a real understanding of the significance of this divine leela of the Lord. Although the pastimes of the Lord are factual history, in each and every pastime, there is a great depth and wealth of philosophy, to enlighten our innermost consciousness in pursuance of the truth. So, it is explained that Mother Yasodha, she was one day Ah! churning the butter. This is taking place in Vrindavan and Bhagwan, the Supreme Absolute Truth had taken the form of a beautiful little baby and he began to grab on to his mother, “Mataji”, he was umm…indicating, “I want milk, give me milk.” Han! So, mother Yasodha was thinking ‘Ah! my beautiful child, she wants... he wants the milk.’ So, she put down the churning rod and she sat down and she began to feed God, Sri Krishna the milk from her body and he was very happy Ah! This is very significant. Mother Yasodha was thinking ‘If I do not feed Krishna milk, he will die.’ She was so overwhelmed with parental affection for the lord that she considered him her own helpless child. Now, God is the master of all living beings. He is sometimes is called upon by name; sarveswareshwareswarah.

There is a song by Bhakti Vinod Thakur tumi sarveśvareśvara, brajendra-kumāra! [Saranagati, Section: Goptritve Varana Song 4]. That the supreme controller of all controllers. Understand that everyone has some control. The father has the control over his family, the mother has the control over the father always, its common knowledge, the fathers don’t like to admit it, usually the women they don’t externally admit it because they don’t want to give away their secrets, but every woman knows that she has control over her husband Ah? and husband has control over his spherical activities and the children they have control over their little spherical toys and little rooms and everything and even the insignificant ant has some domain of his control. So, everyone has some power to control. The demigods, the great devatas, they control entire universal departments. But the supreme controller who is the controller of all other controllers who are controller of everything else. That is Krishna, Brajendranandan-kumara. So, therefore he is the supreme master of all living beings but he is also the supreme enjoyer.

Priority in Life

Philosophically, let us try to understand the nature of God. bhoktāraṁ yajṣa-tapasāṁ sarva-loka-maheśvaram [Bg.5.29]. He is the supreme enjoyer. He is the reservoir of all pleasures. Now, what gives you most enjoyment because after all man is made in the image of God? We are part of God, qualitatively one with God. Let us say a man is a judge in the courtroom, when he goes into the court, everyone approaches, “Your honor, may I now be permitted to speak?” and the judge says [knock voice] “Guilty, go to jail.” “Your Honor, I am sorry.” Ah! The judge is in very, very powerful position. Nobody comes in and says, “Haa… Arvindji, how is it going?” No…it’s, “Your honor Ah! May I be permitted to come to the stand.”, “Yes, you can go.” That is the only way you can approach the judge when he is sitting on his bench. When the judge comes home, do you think his wife is like that, “Your Honor may I be permitted to give you food?” [devotees laugh] No… she says, “Arvind why you are late for dinner? I told you not to be late for dinner. You always keeping me waiting. Now sit down.” He says, “Yes, your honor.” [Maharaj laugh, devotees laugh] Ah? As a sanyasi we can see everything unbiasly [Maharaj laughter, devotees laugh] and the children when they come home, “Your Honor you are home.”, No. they jump on his back and they say, “Let’s play, let’s wrestle, you be the horse and I will ride on you.” Ah! Our Guru Maharaj tells one story about one High Court justice and his one very, very important man, and he was a big, big politician, he was waiting for three hours in the waiting lobby to get into this judge’s chamber to speak to him about some business. He told the secretary, “I have…I am a very big politician and I have a very important business to speak with judge.” She said, “Alright, he is busy in an important meeting, you wait.” So, he was waiting hours and hours and the secretary left, he thought ‘What is this important meeting?’ And he went to the judge’s door and just barely open the door to peak in. This is a true story, and the judge’s child, his son, little boy like this, like Giriraj, the father was playing the part of a horse and the son was on his back playing the part of the rider and he was kicking the father, “Go, go, go.” And the father was going, Tutut…Tutut.. Tutut. [devotees laugh]. And the son would pull his neck and would say, “Ho, ho, ho stop.” The father would stop and then he said kicking under him and say, “Go, go.” and the father would go. And he sees ‘What kind of judge is this? And I am such an important politician he is keeping me thou.. three hours waiting for this?’ The judge says, “Yes of course; your relationship with me is very formal and it not very..it doesn’t give me much enjoyment but my son, Ah! It is love; it is intimate.” So, the relationship of intimate friends, of lovers is far more pleasing to the Lord than the relationship of simply an obedient servant. But you must understand in order to come to that stage of intimate love, you have to become a perfect obedient servant. So, the pastimes of Vrindavan, Mother Yasodha, she was in such an exalted position that Krishna was taking the position that, “Mommy if you do not feed me, I will die.” So she began to feed him her milk. But it is described that day her servant was not in, so she has to do all her household work herself Ah! and the milk that is on the stove from the cows, it began to boil over, so she was thinking Ha! The milk is boiling over, so she put Lord little lord Krishna down and she ran to the stove. He did not like that; he was thinking ‘What is this? Everyone makes other priority above me.’ Ah?. Does that sound familiar? We all have so many priorities that are more important than God. So, mother Yasodha was thinking that milk was a priority beyond God, so she put him down. This is the most unfortunate condition of life for us.

Purpose of Human Life

Human life according to Brahma sutra says athāto brahma jijṣāsā [ The first aphorism in the Vedānta-sūtra], that now that you have attained this rare and valuable human birth, your business is self-realization. yo vā etadakśaraṃ gārgyaviditvāsmāllokātpraiti sa kṛpaṇaḥ [Brhadaranyaka Upanishad 3.8.10] nāyaṁ deho deha-bhājāṁ,nṛloke kaṣṭān kāmān arhate viḍ-bhujāṁ ye [ŚB 5.5.1]

That this human life is very, very rare, [Maharaja laughs] Now that you have attained this rare human life, do not waste it like the cats and dogs, and the hogs that eats stool, simply trying to enjoy your senses and trying to give sense gratification to your family members, your loved ones, your friends and society. The animals are doing that too. There are certain packs of animals where if you attack one of them; the whole pack will come to defend one of its members. Ah? So just because we are extending ourselves to provide and support the sense gratification of others, that is not put us on any higher level than animal civilization. The animals are eating and sleeping and mating and defending. Human civilization has created by its modern technology and scientific advancement, a very, very sophisticated life dedicated to satisfying the senses- eating, sleeping, mating, and defending. The hog is eating the garbage on the street, and he is enjoying it like anything and we are eating in the finest restaurants. What is the difference? The business is to simply to enjoy eating. The monkeys Ahan! Did you ever study the monkeys? They just jump around from branch to branch, having sex as much as possible, no anxieties, no AIDS, no herpes, and no gonorrhea, no syphilis, they don’t have to work nine to five to support the family, they don’t have divorces, haa? They just have sex. They enjoy like anything. So, we are having a sophisticated regulated form of sex life, but the business of enjoyment of this pleasure is the same. Sleeping, Ahan! you work so hard, you have a nice big house, the dogs are sleeping in the streets. In Bombay, there are more humans sleeping in the streets than dogs, because my experience is that humans sleeping in the streets are more happy than most of the people sleeping in the houses Ah? In my country, America, the upper class, eighty percent of the males in the upper-class income bracket the millionaires have to take sleeping pills to sleep at night Ah? Big mansion beautiful security guards, gardens and fences and all electrical devices to keep out burglars, and nice marble floors, [00:31:10 unclear] pillars, beautiful beds with silken sheets, and they go, they lay down and simply roll down in complete anxiety and frustration until they take some drugs to put them to sleep. But, Bombay, Han! such a nice city, what is that one fifth population lives on the streets, someone told me like that Han? [Devotee:] More than one fifth. [Maharaj:] More. Han!. I... I have seen, most of them, when it is time to go to sleep, they just lay down, they just put their arm down and [Maharaja makes snoring voice] [devotees laugh]. No sleeping pills, no rolling around, no anxieties. So, sleep is sleep, whether you are dog in the streets or whether you are President Bush in the White House Ah? And as far as defending, the dog has his teeth, very proud of their teeth. [Maharaja making dogs teeth pressing noise], monkeys have teeth too [Maharaja making monkey teeth pressing voice]. Tigers have claws, porcupines have prickles, skunk has a very bad smell, all these animals have unique way of defending themselves and threatening their potential enemies. So, human beings, big animals, huge gigantic animals, they have nuclear bombs, missiles Han. The Americans, the Russians, did you ever see them? That is building these missiles and it is just the same as animals showing their claws. Just see my missiles [Maharaja making proud expression] I have bigger missiles, Rif... Rif... they try to scare each other, with their fangs and the claws, and if all else fails, they fight. [Maharaja makes animal fighting expressions] Simply animal life. We say, “We are nice people, we work hard nine to five, we don’t hurt anybody, we feed our families, we are nice people.” But the animals do like that too. Did you ever see the birds in the nest? The father is very, he is very honorable citizen, he goes out every morning, and at the break of dawn, he is going to looking for worms to bring home, which is their money he brings it home, feeds the wife, feeds the children, gives them nice nest for them, protects them, very nice. Does that sound familiar? 99.9% of human civilization have no greater integrity than the animals. The only difference between human life and animal life in essence is that a human beings have the power to discriminate and question, “Who am I? Who is God? What is my relationship with God? Why is there suffering? What is the goal of life?”

Until a human being takes sincerely to the path of God consciousness, qualitatively his consciousness is animal. In fact, the Srimad Bhagvatam says that this human civilization is civilization of two legged animals. Now that you have attained this rare human birth. tapo divyaṁ putrakā yena sattvaṁ śuddhyed yasmād brahma-saukhyaṁ tv anantam [SB 5.5.1]. “Take to the path of genuine God consciousness and you will achieve that platform of real happiness, real peace, eternal infinite happiness beyond the flickering pleasures and pains of sense gratification”, But in order to do so you have to make God the priority of our life. We have many responsibilities, do not neglect any of your responsibilities, do not neglect your business or your medical profession or your household affairs, do not neglect your wife or your children, but by no means neglect the needs of your soul. jīvera svarūpa haya kṛṣṇera nitya-dāsa [Cc. Madhya 20.108] We are eternally the servants of Krishna. Human life, your duty, your responsibility, your first and foremost obligation is to learn to love God in this life and to share that love with the world and if we neglect that for any other purpose, we are the most unfortunate beings on earth especially if we know better and we continuously neglect by making everything else our priority. “When everything else is taken care of, if I have any time left over, then I will do something for God.” It should be just the opposite. Therefore, the great saints and sages say you must regulate your life.

rāga-dveṣa-vimuktais tu

viṣayān indriyaiś caran

ātma-vaśyairvidheyātmā

prasādam adhigacchati

[Bg. 2.64]

[But a person free from all attachment and aversion and able to control his senses through regulative principles of freedom can obtain the complete mercy of the Lord.]

Krishna says, “You must follow the regulative principles of freedom; you must regulate your life.”

Every day we should be chanting the names of God which is the recommended process of self-realization .Strictly specific number of times, we should do our puja, and we should read the shastras and most importantly we should regularly associate with saintly persons who influences to inspiration to take to spiritual side of life seriously and by that process God would become the priority of our life and if you water the root of the tree, and God is the root of all existence, then everything will flourish in our life.

Makhanchor

So, Mother Yashoda, she had another priority Ah? She decided ‘I have to take care of the milk.’, So she put Krishna down. So, Krishna... Of course, her making another priority is different than ours, but I am using this as an example, because, ultimately, she was cooking the milk for Krishna Ah? So, she was perfect, Krishna became very angry, “Mat… Mataji, she thinks stirring the milk is more important than feeding me, Ahan! I will teach her a lesson.”

So, he went to a wooden grinding motor which was turned upside down and he climbed upon it and he broke with a rock, the clay pot of butter, and he began to eat it. Ha! he likes butter very much, his name is makhanchor. Makhanchor means he is butter thief. Another question may arise, “Why God is a thief? What type of example, is he setting? He expects us to be honest but he is a thief.”, Han! Actually, is God a thief? Definition of thief is someone who usurps another person’s property but Krishna says in Gita sarva-loka-maheśvaram [Bg. 5.29] that, “Everything is my property, all the universes and all the planets within the universes and everything within those planets is my property alone. I am the creator; I am ultimately the maintainer and the destroyer of everything that exists.” Everything is the property of God. Knowledge is to know in truth, we own nothing. Han? Ownership means control. If you own something you have complete control over your property. My question is what do you really have control over? The government can come at any time and take over everything you have. In many countries, this happens. And God in the form of time, Kalarupa, will most certainly take away everything you have. Your wealth, your family, your educational qualifications that you are so proud of, your power to speak, your power to think, your power to act. Krishna says, “I am the intelligence of intelligent, I am the ability of the man, I am the strength of the strong, and I am also death personified that takes everything away, including your very dear body.” Which means that it is all his property, he has control over it, it is not yours. Whatever we have in this world is only on temporary lease Han? So, therefore when Krishna steals butter, it is only a pastime stealing, actually that butter belongs to him Han? So just to please his devotees, he appears, he pretends to be a thief, but actually it’s just like a dramatic performance. Because the butter is his, he is not stealing it, he is taking his property; he has every right to it, so in this way makhanchor, navanit-taskara. He began to steal and eat the butter, Han! some people are very afraid of Krishna because of this Han? It is true. People think that ‘Krishna he is Hari, if you worship him, sometimes he takes things away from you, and my God I don’t want anything to taken away from me.’, Hari means Krishna will steal away all your inauspicious qualities from your heart and he will fill your heart with love and knowledge and happiness and peace. Actually, the goal of life is to attract this beautiful makhanchor to steal our hearts. Han? The great saints and sages, they don’t pray, “Krishna give me money. Give me beautiful wife. Give me opulence. Give me power. Give me a good house.” Where do you ever hear the saints, the sadhus praying like this? The great devotees of the Lord they are praying, “makhanchor please come steal my heart, now my heart is the property of so many things of this world which is causing me so much frustration and anxiety. My heart is now stolen by lust and greed and envy and pride and illusion. Please my lord, steal my heart and you enjoy it.” Han? But you see, before Krishna will take pleasure in stealing your heart, first your heart has to become soft like butter, Krishna doesn’t have pleasure in hard hearts, we have hard hearts, that’s the problem and everyone here has hard hearts. Through the process of bhakti, our heart becomes soft like butter and when they become very soft, then Haa… Ba... we will see baby Krishna, he will come Ah., “I want your heart for my pleasure.” And then our life is full of bliss. The supreme goal is to be enjoyed by God.

Mother Yashoda’s Selfless Love for Krishna

So, Krishna began to steal the butter and Mother Yashoda after stirring the milk, she came back, and she saw, “Where is my Gopala? He has gone Han!” She began to look around, “He must be doing some mischief.” And Krishna saw her and he began to hide and she saw the broken pot of butter and she said, “Ahan! just see the mischief. He is doing.” And she picked up a little stick thinking I... ‘He is doing these naughty activities, he may hurt himself, I must teach him.’ When little child Gopala when he saw Kris… Mother Yasodha with whipping stick in hand, he became very frightened, he began to tremble, tears, eyes became red and mother Yashoda was looking all over and she saw him hiding behind the pots and she came to him and he began to run away and she began to ran after him. And as she was chasing him, Han! she had a very thin waist and very big hips and large other parts of body, so it’s difficult for her to run fast, but Krishna he was running very fast. So, she was running and her garlands were falling from her hair and she began to perspire, and when Krishna saw ‘How much Mother Yasodha wants to protect me.”, He allowed himself to be captured by his mother. Please know that the gyanis, they try to capture God through studying the shastra birth after birth after birth after birth and they never catch him. But mother Yasodha by her sweet and simple love, she was able to capture the Supreme Absolute Truth. Han? And as she was thinking, ‘What will I do? I have so many household affairs to take care of, my child in a very naughty mood, he may get hurt. I must tie him up.’ And she took the rope and went to tie it around his waist and the rope is two inches too short and she got more rope and she tied it to the original rope and she put it around his waist and it is two inches to short and then She look around and she fetch more rope and she tied it to the original ropes and she went to put it around his waist and it was two inches to short and then more rope all the ropes in the neighborhood she was collecting. She went to put it around the little child’s waist, small little waist and guess what, it was two inches to short. She began to perspire. Know this, how is it that Krishna is so much full of his own mother? When he saw his mother’s intimate desire to protect him and serve him and love him, he agreed to be tied around the waist by his mother. Don’t take this cheaply, don’t think [00.48.38-00.48.40 inaudible] that we can just tie Krishna around his waist. The fact is mother Yasodha, her love for Krishna, it was so deep, it was so pure, that she would be willing to end her life within a second, just to remove ones part of perspiration from the head of her son, if she thought it was inconvenience to, he... him. At any moment without consideration of her own life to protect her son from the slightest inconvenience, she was absolutely completely merged in only one thought, the pleasure of Krishna. And because she had such pure unmotivated, uninterrupted selfless, love therefore she was qualified to hold a whipping stick before Krishna and make him shake. She was qualified to bind that rope around him, that rope is the personification of bhakti. Krishna is known as bhakt vatsala, that he is captured, he is bound by the love of his devotee. sādhavo hṛdayaṁ mahyaṁ,sādhūnāṁ hṛdayaṁ tv aham [SB 9.4.68].

Krishna said, “My sadhu is my life and soul. The pure devotee, because he has given up everything for my sake, and therefore I give up everything for the sake of my devotee. Because my devotees think of no one and nothing but me. I cannot help but thinking of anything or anyone except my devotee.” That is the nature of love. Mother Yashoda had that love, and therefore, she was able to capture Krishna, and this is the goal of life. By becoming attracted to Krishna, we attract Krishna and this is the goal of life.

Attracted to Krishna

Now how do we become attracted to Krishna in our life? Krishna says janma karma ca me divyam evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ [Bg. 4.9] It is very simple. By associating with saintly persons whose only business is to hear about the Lord, to glorify the Lord, and to sacrifice their lives to serving the Lord. By associating with such souls, the inclination within our own hearts to glorify God is awakened.

Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, the Yuga avatar, he has given a simple formula

nitya-siddhakṛṣṇa-prema`sādhya’kabhunaya

śravaṇādi-śuddha-citte karaye udaya

[Caitanya-Carittamrta Madhya 22.107]

[Pure love for Kṛṣṇa is eternally established in the hearts of the living entities. It is not something to be gained from another source. When the heart is purified by hearing and chanting, this love naturally awakens]

‘That pure love of God is within the heart of every living being, it is your nature to love God. By associating with lovers of God and by chanting the holy names of God, this love is awakened.’ In this age, the process of chanting the holy names of the Lord under the instruction of the great souls is the simple and sublime process, by which anyone in any walk of life can become a pure lover of Lord.

Whether you are a sanyasi gṛhe thāko, vane thāko, sadā 'hari' bole' ḍāko [Gitavali] or whether you are a family man. We have here an Architect, a Lawyer, a Doctor, another Doctor, Mr. Hrishikesh Mafatlal, a very, very responsible business man, thirty thousand employees, eighteen factories at least. We have Dr N.D. Desai Ah! he has over ten thousand employees, international business, we have a Stock Exchange Ana... Analyst. I think something like that, we have a Dietitian, a Dentist, Doctors. There are many people right hear with us tonight, they have families, they have children, wives, parents and they are perfectly responsible to every one of them. They neglect not a single one of their responsibilities in this world. They are very productive, ambitious and creative people in their field of work. But you will find in their lives of all of them that their priority is the service of God, putting God in the center. Making all of their action, all of their lives a form of worship. People dit… talk about how work is worship? For Bhagavad Gita explains work is only worship if it is done in devotion to the Lord and the way we can establish this consciousness within our hearts.

Chaitanya mahaprabhu has described that simply by regular association with saintly persons and sincerely chanting the holy names of God. [devotees chant together] hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare/hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare.

There are many names of God and each name of God, nāmnām akāri bahudhā nija-sarva-śaktis [Siksastaka] is empowered by all the glories and all the strength the purifying power of God himself Han? In the Vedas, the Hare Krishna mahamantra, a specially recommended in this age of kali, this age of quarrel and hypocrisy. And through the process of chanting the names of God and through the process of associating with saintly persons, and giving up unwanted sinful activities, gradually this natural inclination to love God, to know God awakens within our heart. and in the process of that awakening, we can experience that eternal, blissful nature of the self and when we by the grace of God awaken into a healthy natural condition again, then we never have to suffer repetition of birth, death, old age disease again and we realize the eternal, blissful nature of the soul.

janma karma ca me divyam

evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ

tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma

naiti mām eti so ’rjuna

[Bg. 4.9]

[One who knows the transcendental nature of My appearance and activities does not, upon leaving the body, take his birth again in this material world, but attains My eternal abode, O Arjuna.]

Through this sublime process of Harinam kirtan and Sadhu Sanga, we, from within the transcendental nature of the Lord’s activities and his appearance is understood, it is revealed to us.

This beautiful story that we have narrated tonight, when we learn to take pleasure in these stories, when you love someone, there is no greater happiness in hearing the glories of your beloved Han? Just like a parent, if the child does something outstanding, there is no greater happiness to the parents than hearing the glories of their child. If the parent himself is glorified that is not as endearing as when the child is glorified. That is the nature of love. As we develop our love of God, the glorification, the past... of the pastimes and the names of God becomes the ultimate source of pleasure. One time our Guru Maharaj was asked, “Swamiji, what do you hope to get out of all this chanting of Hare Krishna mantra? What is the end? What is the goal?” And our Guru Maharaj replied, “The Supreme goal of chanting Hare Krishna is chanting Hare Krishna.” Because in love, it is the greatest pleasure, it is the infinite ecstasy of the soul and through this process, we never take birth again in this material world but we re... return to the eternal natural position at the lotus feet in the loving service of the Supreme Lord.

Thank you very much.

Hare Krishna.

Is there any questions? These are questions?

Q&A Transcription

[Maharaj reads Question:] What is the need and meaning of coming together and praying together in gatherings?

[Maharaj:] Han! It is said in the Bible by Lord Jesus, “Wherever two or more of my devotees comes together in my name, I am there.” And lord Vishnu, Narayan spoke to Narada Muni, tatra tiṣṭhāmi nārada yatra gāyanti mad-bhaktāḥ [Padma Purāṇa]. “You will not find me in the caves with the yogis doing their meditation, you will not find me in research institutions where the jṣānīs are studying the scriptures, you will find me in only one place, wherever my devotees gather together to chant my names and glories.”

mac-cittā mad-gata-prāṇā

bodhayantaḥ parasparam

kathayantaś ca māṁ nityaṁ

tuṣyanti ca ramanti ca

[Bg. 10.9]

[The thoughts of My pure devotees dwell in Me, their lives are fully devoted to My service, and they derive great satisfaction and bliss from always enlightening one another and conversing about Me.]

Krishna says, “My devotees take great satisfaction and bliss coming together and discussing my glories.” We see that the tendencies that are at home, that we take things very much for granted. Actually Vasudev, Garg Muni came to visit Vasudev and Vasudev was a grihastha. He said, “We are fortunate that you have come to our home.” Because the nature of family man is that they become so much engrossed in their family affairs, they become so much engrossed in their business affairs, that their spiritual life becomes second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth priority. Therefore, there is an absolute need to associate with saintly persons, otherwise our worship at home becomes ritualistic, it becomes a tradition. It is the association of saints that maintain and preserve the life and essence within the spiritual practices. So, therefore, it is essential that you do continue your prayers and your worship at home, but it is as essential that you regularly come together with saintly persons to get renewed inspiration and conviction and it is also your duty to come together in gatherings to share the realization you have with others. A householder is meant, whatever God realization he has, he is meant to give that in charity to others, he is meant to inspire others, the more you have, the more you should give and the more you give, the more you obtain, that is the spiritual principle, so both are essential. Our puja at home on a very regulated and strict basis with our family members and also the association of saintly persons and gatherings where we come together to glorify the Holy Name.

[Maharaj reads Question:] What is the difference between Allah, Jehovah, Jesus and Krishna?

[Maharaj:] It is like the difference between Surya, Sol and the sun Han! In Hindi, you call the sun ‘Surya’, In Mexico, they call it sol, In English, we call it the sun. They are different names, in different languages for the same sun. God has many names, but God is one.

[Maharaj reads Question:] Many Indians are pious and they pray to the Lord, but they don’t exhibit their devotion. The question is why do you put tilak, carrying chanting beads openly and do loud kirtans? What is the need of show?

[Maharaj:] Ahan! It is not show, at least this is not supposed to be show. It is charity. You see Mr. Desai, Mr. Mafatlal, Han! Vishvarupa, he wears tilak, some wear tilak all the time. Now Mr. Mafatlal and Vishwarupa are wearing tilak, when they go to work, they may not wear tilak. They don’t have the shaved heads, they... when they go to work, they don’t wear clothes like this. You hav.. you wear what is practical Han! Our Doctor Nagarkatti, Sri Govind, he gives lecture, he has nice flowing hairs and he has tie and nice distinguished Amm... dress shirt that he wears, Han! But he speaks the philosophy of Bhagavad Gita, and he convinces many people. So, you see it is not a question of changing clothes, it is a question of changing hearts. But just like police men wears a uniform because it has a purpose, so a sadhu, one who is sanyasi or brahmachari, his only business is to help and inspire others to become God conscious. So, these shaved head, the tilak, and the robes is a uniform which is helpful for the purpose of identifying ourselves as persons who are distributing the word of God. And when people see, they are reminding of god and they can easily find us and question us. It helps our service to them, but it is not necessary, the real necessary ingredient is the sincere desire within our heart, the chanting of Holy Names.

[Maharaj reads Question:] In today’s world, the competition is so severe, Han! very severe to maintain and sustain ourselves, how can a man of today’s modern world afford to be a devotee?

[Maharaj:] Han!. Arjuna was a very good competitor and he was the best devotee, correct? It is not that when you become a devotee, you become impotent, uncreative, and unproductive and of everyone in the battle field of Kurukshetra, Arjuna was the most creative, the most important and the most productive, why? because he was empowered by God. He had a clear and pure mind and why was he empowered by God? Because he was the devotee of God. This is something you may say, well, “Arjuna, he was five thousand years ago, it was a long back and it is all in scriptures, what is that have to do with today’s life?” Han! Dr Desai, if you analyze his business, he has a very competitive business, the textile industry, Mr. Mafata Lal, very competitive business, and they are excelling and practically above all other competitors, but you see they are motivated by mercy, compassion and love, and not of greed and lust and pride and therefore they are finding fulfillment. Arjuna was a competitor and Duryodhana was a competitor, the fact that Arjuna was a devotee of Krishna didn’t make him less than Duryodhana, it made him more. but the difference was Duryodhana was motivated to compete on the basis of his own selfish greed. And Arjuna was motivated to compete by love, by mercy, by compassion, he was working for the benefit of all living beings. Duryodhana was exploiting all living beings. If you become a devotee of lord, you will become most efficient and most expert.

[Maharaj reads Question:] Han! We find in Mahabharata serial, Pandavas even after being virtuous in religion, they were suffering a lot. So, what is the use of devotion if in course of time we are going to suffer like them?

[Maharaj:] Ahan! [Maharaja Laughs] You see, Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, “What tastes like poison in the beginning, taste like nectar in the end and what tastes like nectar in the beginning, taste like poison in the end.” Duryodhana by his sinful activities immediately, he was enjoying, he had the control of the world for 14 years, Han? he was enjoying like anything, why? because he was a sinful rascal, [devotees laugh] that’s the reason. He cheated the Pandavas in a gambling match, he tried to kill them, he just took everything away by illegal means and he was enjoying and the Pandavas they were honest, they were virtuous, they lost everything, it appeared they were suffering, they were not suffering, because they were always remembering the Krishna, they were experiencing the love of God within their heart even without anything. But what was the end result? The end result was that the Pandavas were given the kingdom of earth, and Duryodhana died a horrible miserable death. So, by sinful acts, you may immediately have a sense of enjoyment for some time but you cannot be sure that your karma will fructify your miserable horrible suffering death and in the next life you have to pay the hellish consequences of all your sins. You see, this material world is the place of suffering for everyone. If you tolerate that suffering in the service of God, the destination is freedom from suffering forever. If you tolerate the sufferings for your own sense gratification, the fruit is simply birth after birth after birth of increased suffering. Krishna says, “One who is illumine from within, who enjoys within, who takes pleasure within; he is a man of real intelligence.” The Pandavas were constantly thinking and remembering of Krishna and therefore whether they had everything or nothing, they were at peace. Whereas Duryodhana, whether he was the king of world, even at that stage, he was frustrated, envious person who wanted more. Correct? So, if become a devotee of the lord, you will be completely freed of all sufferings, any situations and in the end, you are guaranteed to be victorious; this is the lesson of Mahabharata.

[Maharaj reads Question:] How to find the golden mean between all the religious faith in today's world?

[Maharaj:] The only process is to take shelter of a self-realized soul who understands the essence of religion. The essence of religion is to love God. All sectarian differences are only due to those misconceptions which causes to forget the essence i.e., love of God. When we become too much attached to the names, the forms, particular style of ritual and we forget the purpose and spirit behind it, then we become sectarian, we become jealous and we become very close minded. But if we hear from a truly self-realized soul who has imbibed within his heart the real essence from the scriptures and from the previous great souls and he is presenting pure love, devotional service, surrender, then we transcend all sectarian differences. Krishna says in Gita.

sarva-dharman parityajya

mam ekam saranam vraja

aham tvam sarva-papebhyo

mksayisyami ma sucah

[ Bg 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions and do not fear.]

‘Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender to me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions and do not fear.’ When we find that great soul by the mercy of Krishna. if we are sincere, it is said by the mercy of Krishna one gets Guru and by the mercy of Guru one gets Krishna. If we are sincere, the Lord within our hearts will direct us to Guru and the real and Bonafide Guru, he will teach pure surrender to Krishna, pure love, pure devotion as the goal of life. He will teach the synthesis of all religious philosophies through the process of surrender, he will teach us that, by chanting the name of God, we can realize the ultimate goal of all religion. Pure bhakti, pure devotion and then that all merciful qualities of soul, we will be compassionate and infinitely humble and ready to serve all living beings. That is the goal of life. That is what is available for every one of us. So, my humble request is that we take this opportunity that God has given us most seriously in our life and become peaceful and share that peace with the world.

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
